data.title: HSBC secures first stablecoin license in Hong Kong from HKMA, alongside Anchorpoint Financial. A milestone for regulated digital assets in Asia.
data.description: In a significant development, Hong Kong has issued its first stablecoin issuer licenses to HSBC and Anchorpoint Financial. This marks a crucial milestone for the regulation of digital assets in Asia. This move signifies the growing acceptance and recognition of stablecoins in the region. This decision allows both companies to operate legally in the stablecoin market, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. The issuance of these licenses reflects Hong Kong's commitment to fostering a secure and transparent digital asset ecosystem. This decision is expected to have a profound impact on the digital currency sector, with HSBC and Anchorpoint Financial spearheading the way with the newfound licenses.

data.title: David Ellison's Paramount Skydance Locks In Funding For Massive Warner Bros. Discovery Buyout
data.description: Paramount SkyDance (NASDAQ: PSKY) has successfully obtained permanent financing for its acquisition of Warner Bros Discovery (NASDAQ: WBD), reducing its debt commitments from $54 billion to $49 billion. This financing was secured from a group of 18 banks, which syndicated the bridge loan, and established $5 billion in senior term loans along with a $5 billion revolving credit facility, while eliminating a $3.5 billion credit line. These loans are backed by a first-priority claim on all assets, encompassing Paramount Global, Skydance Media, and Warner Bros., post-merger completion. This funding deal ranks among the largest debt packages of the current year for the combined entity.

data.title: Swedish Court Reschedules Delivery of Judgment in PriceRunner vs Google Antitrust Case
data.description: Klarna Group plc (NYSE: KLAR) recently announced that the Patent and Market Court in Stockholm, Sweden has delayed the delivery of its verdict in the antitrust damages case filed by PriceRunner, a subsidiary of Klarna, against Google. The court has moved the publication of its judgment from April 15 to June 10, 2026, scheduled to be released at 11:00 CET. The court cited the need for more time to finalize the judgment in this case.
